subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear, focused and testable research question
Adequate explanation of key concepts and rationale for COâ measurement
Appropriate range of methods with some justification (e.g., choice of COâ probe, incubation temperature)
weaknesses: Explanations are occasionally repetitive or vague rather than deeply analytical
Control design (no time-matched water control per run) is weak
Rationale for methodological choices sometimes superficial (e.g., stopping criterion based on âefficiencyâ)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation communicates the topic adequately, with a clearly stated, focused research question that aligns with an experimentally testable issue (band 5 for question formulation). Methodology is appropriate and generally complete, although some controls and rationales lack depth (band 3â4). Overall performance merits a mid-band score of 3/6, reflecting adequate communication and method but not the comprehensive accuracy and depth required for higher marks.

strengths: Use of relevant scientific terminology (e.g., anaerobic respiration, ppm, uncertainty)
Application of source material to justify experimental conditions (temperature, concentrations)
weaknesses: Application of literature is mostly descriptive with no critical integration
Background conflates fermentation and respiration, indicating conceptual confusion
Key citations (e.g., NIH yeast history) lack proper in-text and bibliographic details
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The use of source material is generally relevant but remains largely descriptive, with limited critical integration (band 3â4 at lower end). Terminology is mostly accurate but occasionally inconsistent. This level of performance corresponds to a low mid-band score of 2/6, reflecting partially effectiveâbut not fully coherentâknowledge use.

strengths: Data processing shows basic mean and uncertainty calculations
Argument follows a logical overall structure linking back to the research question
weaknesses: Evaluation is superficial with few supported conclusions and no statistical rigour
Unsupported leaps in discussion (e.g., asserting cell death from potassium sorbate without evidence)
No quantitative error analysis or discussion of measurement precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis and discussion demonstrate only limited critical thinking. While the structure is logical, evaluation lacks depth, statistical support and coherent argument development (bands 1â3). The reasoning is minimal, meriting a low score of 1/12.

strengths: Clear overall structure with numbered sections, table of contents and works-cited list
Consistent layout of headings, tables and graphs; inclusion of required elements (title, word count, page numbers)
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting in the works-cited list
Minor typographical issues (uneven table borders, stray symbols)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is generally clear and appropriately structured (band 3), but minor formatting inconsistencies and citation errors prevent full marks. A mid-band score of 2/4 reflects correct layout and structure with room for improvement in detailed formatting.

strandSelections.reasoning: The EE follows the IB structure with clear numbered sections, appendices and works-cited list.  All required elements appear to be present and logically ordered, satisfying the criteria for a clear and appropriate structure.

Headings, tables and graphs are formatted consistently; word count, title and page numbers are included.  Minor typographical issues (uneven table borders, stray symbols) are present but overall layout conventions are correctly applied.

The argument follows a logical structure (introduction â method â results â discussion) and usually links back to the RQ, but coherence is disrupted by repetition and occasional unsupported leaps.  This represents an adequate yet inconsistent argument, meeting the descriptor for 4â6 but meriting the lower score within that band.

The introduction explains yeast respiration, each substance, and why CO2 is recorded. Explanations are mostly correct but occasionally repetitive or vague (e.g., conflating respiration with ânormal respiration would be inhibitedâ). Overall clarity is acceptable and readers understand the investigation, yet depth is limited and some passages are verbose rather than explanatory, matching an âadequately communicatedâ standard rather than the comprehensive detail expected in the top band.

Literature is cited to choose concentrations and temperature, and some links are made to data interpretation.  Nevertheless, most sources are used descriptively; no critical integration or comparison is attempted.  Application is therefore âmostly relevantâ but only partially effective, aligning with the 3â4 band at the lower end.

The student calculates means, uncertainties and plots concentration-response curves, showing basic analytical treatment that is clearly linked to the RQ.  Yet statistical rigour is limited (no significance testing), and plateau decisions reduce data integrity.  Analysis is therefore adequate but not sophisticated, fitting the 4â6 band at its lower edge.

A full procedure, clear variable table, five-trial design and justification of concentration ranges are provided.  Selection of a COâ probe and 32 Â°C incubation are rationalised with literature.  However, some controls are weak (no true time-matched water control during each run) and rationale is occasionally superficial (e.g., âefficiencyâ used to justify terminating runs early).  The range of methods is appropriate but not fully optimised, so the work sits solidly in the 3â4 band.

Conclusions largely restate observable trends; evaluation table lists strengths and limitations but offers few in-depth scientific explanations.  Support for claims (e.g., potassium sorbate âyeast will dieâ) is weak.  The work shows a basic, partly supported evaluation consistent with the upper end of the 1â3 band.

The research question is explicitly stated, narrowly focused (specific concentrations, single species, single response variable) and entirely appropriate for Biology.  The RW ties directly to an experimentally testable issue.  Minor ambiguity (e.g., phrasing begins with âTo what extent â¦ affect concentration of CO2 producedâ) does not prevent clear focus, so the descriptor for the top band is largely met.

Terminology such as âanaerobic respirationâ, âppmâ, âuncertaintyâ, and âline of best fitâ is generally accurate.  Occasional slips (e.g., calling respiration âfermentation involving oxygenâ, grammatical confusion) prevent consistent precision.  This represents mostly accurate but not fully consistent use.

annotations.comment: The presence of the title at the very start satisfies the requirement for clearly stating the investigationâs title.
Including the word count on the title page meets the formatting criterion and confirms the essay is within the 4000-word limit.
The research question is focused, testable and clearly stated, specifying species, reactants and measurement method.
Consider rephrasing âTo what extent â¦ affect concentration of Carbon Dioxide producedâ to âHow do the specified concentrations of â¦ affect the COâ output?â for clarity.
Background conflates fermentation and respiration. Clarify that anaerobic respiration (fermentation) occurs without Oâ and is distinct from ventilation.
Variables are listed in text but could be organized in a table for clarity and ease of reference.
Hypothesis 1 predicts direction correctly but could specify expected % change in COâ to improve testability.
In the safety section, explain disposal steps for potassium sorbate per MSDS to strengthen environmental considerations.
The reference to the NIH for yeast history lacks citation details. Provide a proper in-text citation and full bibliographic entry for credibility.
Description of sodium chlorideâs effect is superficial. Explain osmotic stress on yeast cells and its impact on metabolic rate.
Limitations of the COâ probe are noted but lack quantitative error analysis. Include response time, calibration range and measurement precision.
The methodology states runs stop at plateau (<100âppm change) which may bias max values. Suggest full 5âmin runs or standardized time cut-off.
Figure 2âs axes lack units in the caption. Label x as concentration (%) and y as COâ concentration (ppm) for clarity.
The line-of-best-fit is shown but the equation and RÂ² are missing. Include these to support trend analysis.
Discussion refers to âcorrelationâ without specifying coefficient or p-value. Provide actual statistical measure or discuss limitations of correlation analysis.
Extrapolating exponential trends beyond measured concentrations may be misleading. Qualify predictions with caution about data range.
Conclusion restates findings but does not critically assess hypothesis support or discuss alternative explanations. Add evaluative commentary.
The evaluation table clearly lists strengths but could link improvements back to research question to show integrated reflection.
Stopping the COâ readings at plateau can reduce reproducibility. Recommend recording the full time series for each trial.
The procedural description ensures reproducibility by using precise tools and clear steps, strengthening the experimental reliability.
Citation styles in the Works Cited are inconsistent (varying DOI formats and capitalization). Apply a uniform citation style (e.g., APA).
Raw data tables include plateau markers (*) but do not link back to methodology criteria. Clarify plateau definition in methods.
Outlier in sucrose 65% trial average (5580âppm) deviates strongly. Investigate experimental error or probe malfunction.
Processed data tables clearly show mean and uncertainty calculations, demonstrating effective data handling.
The table of contents provides a clear structure with numbered sections, meeting presentation requirements.
